So Bitcoin hits a million and suddenly every central bank on Earth decides to pull the emergency brake like they're all riding the same financial roller coaster. Because nothing says 'we're in control' quite like collectively panicking at the exact same moment.

Apparently when Bitcoin goes parabolic, so does everyone's electricity bill as mining operations multiply faster than rabbits in springtime. Who knew that digital money required the power consumption of a small country to validate made-up transactions?

China bans crypto for the thousandth time this decade and somehow their currency gets stronger, which is totally not suspicious timing at all. It's almost like they've been planning this move since before Bitcoin even existed, but that would be crazy conspiracy thinking.

Graphics cards become more precious than gold again because Karen from accounting needs to build a mining rig with her stimulus check. Meanwhile, actual gamers are stuck playing Minecraft on their Nokia flip phones because progress.

Kids are dropping out of college faster than hot potatoes to become crypto day traders, because why get an education when you can just gamble your future away? Universities are probably thrilled to lose 15% of their revenue stream to digital Monopoly money.

Mental health professionals are making bank treating people addicted to watching numbers go up and down on screens all day. The real conspiracy is whether these therapists are secretly buying Bitcoin with their increased consultation fees.

Some burrito joint owner gets rich off Bitcoin and suddenly thinks he's running a Michelin star establishment by charging FORTY SEVEN DOLLARS for what is essentially beans wrapped in a tortilla, and you know what, that's the final straw, that's where I draw the line, this is absolutely unacceptable gouging of working people who just want affordable lunch!
